# ppforest2

ppforest2 provides projection pursuit oblique decision trees and random
forests for classification. Instead of splitting on single variables,
each node projects the data onto a linear combination of features,
capturing structure that axis-aligned trees miss.

The package wraps a high-performance C++ core and is intended as a
modern successor to
[`PPforest`](https://cran.r-project.org/package=PPforest).

Key capabilities: oblique splits via projection pursuit, multi-threaded
forest training (OpenMP), cross-platform reproducibility, three variable
importance measures (projection-based, weighted, permutation), LDA/PDA
optimisation, OOB error estimation, and
[parsnip](https://parsnip.tidymodels.org/) / tidymodels integration.

## Installation

``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("andres-vidal/ppforest2", subdir = "bindings/R", build = FALSE)
```

## Usage

### Single tree

``` r
library(ppforest2)

model <- pptr(Species ~ ., data = iris)
predict(model, iris[1:5, ])
summary(model)
```

### Random forest

``` r
forest <- pprf(Species ~ ., data = iris, size = 500)
predict(forest, iris[1:5, ])
predict(forest, iris[1:5, ], type = "prob")   # vote proportions
summary(forest)
```

### Regularisation (PDA)

When classes are highly correlated or the number of variables is large
relative to the sample size, penalised discriminant analysis can improve
separation:

``` r
pptr(Species ~ ., data = iris, lambda = 0.5)
```

### Visualisation

ppforest2 provides four diagnostic plot types (requires
[ggplot2](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/)):

``` r
# Mosaic overview: structure + importance + boundaries
plot(model)

# Individual plot types
plot(model, type = "structure")     # tree diagram with per-node histograms
plot(model, type = "importance")    # variable importance bar chart
plot(model, type = "projection")    # projected data at each split
plot(model, type = "boundaries")    # decision boundaries in feature space

# Forest: importance across all trees, or inspect individual trees
plot(forest)
plot(forest, type = "structure", tree_index = 1)
plot(forest, type = "boundaries", tree_index = 1)
```

### tidymodels integration

ppforest2 integrates with [parsnip](https://parsnip.tidymodels.org/):

``` r
library(parsnip)

# Single tree
spec <- pp_tree(lambda = 0) |> set_engine("ppforest2") |> set_mode("classification")
fit  <- fit(spec, Species ~ ., data = iris)

# Random forest
spec <- pp_rand_forest(trees = 50, mtry = 2) |> set_engine("ppforest2")
fit  <- spec |> fit(Species ~ ., data = iris)
predict(fit, iris, type = "prob")
```

### JSON serialisation

Models can be saved and loaded in JSON format, enabling interoperability
with the C++ CLI and other language bindings:

``` r
save_json(model, "model.json")
restored <- load_json("model.json")
```

## Learning more

- [`vignette("introduction")`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/articles/introduction.md)
  — a tutorial covering trees, forests, visualisation, and tidymodels
  integration.
- [C++ API
  Reference](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/cpp/) —
  core library documentation (Doxygen).
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/andres-vidal/ppforest2) —
  source code, build instructions, and benchmarks.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`NCI60`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/NCI60.md)
  : NCI60 Dataset

- [`bag_samples()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/bag_samples.md)
  : In-bag row indices per tree.

- [`binarize_disabled()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/binarize_disabled.md)
  : Disabled binarization strategy (placeholder).

- [`binarize_largest_gap()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/binarize_largest_gap.md)
  : Largest-gap binarization strategy.

- [`california_housing`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/california_housing.md)
  : California Housing Dataset

- [`crab`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/crab.md)
  : Australian Crabs Dataset

- [`crabs`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/crabs.md)
  : Crabs Dataset

- [`cutpoint_mean_of_means()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/cutpoint_mean_of_means.md)
  : Mean-of-means split cutpoint strategy.

- [`fishcatch`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/fishcatch.md)
  : Fish Catch Dataset

- [`fitted(`*`<ppmodel>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/fitted.ppmodel.md)
  : Fitted (in-sample) predictions from a ppforest2 model.

- [`formula(`*`<ppmodel>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/formula.ppmodel.md)
  : Formula extractor for ppforest2 models.

- [`glass`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/glass.md)
  : Glass Dataset

- [`grouping_by_cutpoint()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/grouping_by_cutpoint.md)
  : Cutpoint-based grouping strategy (regression).

- [`grouping_by_label()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/grouping_by_label.md)
  : Label-based grouping strategy.

- [`image`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/image.md)
  : Image Dataset

- [`leaf_majority_vote()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/leaf_majority_vote.md)
  : Majority-vote leaf strategy.

- [`leaf_mean_response()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/leaf_mean_response.md)
  : Mean-response leaf strategy.

- [`leukemia`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/leukemia.md)
  : Leukemia Dataset

- [`load_json()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/load_json.md)
  : Load a model from a JSON file.

- [`lymphoma`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/lymphoma.md)
  : Lymphoma Dataset

- [`nobs(`*`<ppmodel>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/nobs.ppmodel.md)
  : Number of observations used to fit a ppforest2 model.

- [`olive`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/olive.md)
  : Olive Dataset

- [`oob_error()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/oob_error.md)
  : Out-of-bag error for a random forest.

- [`oob_predictions()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/oob_predictions.md)
  : Out-of-bag predictions for a random forest.

- [`oob_samples()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/oob_samples.md)
  : Out-of-bag row indices per tree.

- [`parkinson`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/parkinson.md)
  : Parkinson Dataset

- [`permuted_importance()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/permuted_importance.md)
  : Permuted variable importance for a random forest.

- [`plot(`*`<pprf>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/plot.pprf.md)
  : Plot a pprf model.

- [`plot(`*`<pptr>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/plot.pptr.md)
  : Plot a pptr model.

- [`pp_pda()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/pp_pda.md)
  : PDA projection pursuit strategy.

- [`pp_rand_forest()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/pp_rand_forest.md)
  : Parsnip model specification for pprf.

- [`pp_tree()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/pp_tree.md)
  : Parsnip model specification for pptr.

- [`pprf()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/pprf.md)
  : Trains a Random Forest of Projection-Pursuit oblique decision trees.

- [`pptr()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/pptr.md)
  : Trains a Projection-Pursuit oblique decision tree.

- [`predict(`*`<pprf_classification>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/predict.pprf_classification.md)
  : Predicts labels or vote proportions from a pprf model
  (classification mode).

- [`predict(`*`<pprf_regression>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/predict.pprf_regression.md)
  : Predicts numeric responses from a pprf model (regression mode).

- [`predict(`*`<pptr_classification>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/predict.pptr_classification.md)
  : Predicts labels or per-group one-hot proportions from a pptr model
  (classification mode).

- [`predict(`*`<pptr_regression>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/predict.pptr_regression.md)
  : Predicts numeric responses from a pptr model (regression mode).

- [`print(`*`<pprf>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/print.pprf.md)
  : Prints a compact summary of a pprf forest.

- [`print(`*`<pptr>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/print.pptr.md)
  : Prints the structure of a pptr tree.

- [`projection_importance()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/projection_importance.md)
  : Projection-coefficient variable importance.

- [`residuals(`*`<ppmodel>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/residuals.ppmodel.md)
  : Residuals from a regression ppforest2 model.

- [`save_json()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/save_json.md)
  : Save a model to a JSON file.

- [`stop_any()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/stop_any.md)
  : Composite stopping rule (logical OR).

- [`stop_max_depth()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/stop_max_depth.md)
  : Maximum-depth stopping rule.

- [`stop_min_size()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/stop_min_size.md)
  : Minimum-size stopping rule.

- [`stop_min_variance()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/stop_min_variance.md)
  : Minimum-variance stopping rule.

- [`stop_pure_node()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/stop_pure_node.md)
  : Pure-node stopping rule.

- [`summary(`*`<pprf>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/summary.pprf.md)
  : Summary of a pprf forest (shared header + VI).

- [`update(`*`<pp_rand_forest>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/update.pp_rand_forest.md)
  :

  Update a `pp_rand_forest` model specification.

- [`update(`*`<pp_tree>`*`)`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/update.pp_tree.md)
  :

  Update a `pp_tree` model specification.

- [`vars_all()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/vars_all.md)
  : All-variables selection strategy.

- [`vars_uniform()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/vars_uniform.md)
  : Uniform random variable selection strategy.

- [`weighted_importance()`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/weighted_importance.md)
  : Weighted projection variable importance for a random forest.

- [`wine`](https://andres-vidal.github.io/ppforest2/v0.1.1/r/reference/wine.md)
  : Wine Dataset
